answer:
Let \( x_{i} = \frac{\pi}{2} + \frac{i}{2n}\pi \), \( i = 1,2,\ldots,n \).

Then, \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \).

By the identity \( \sin ^{2} x + \cos ^{2} x = 1 \), we have \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \cos ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \).

Using the identity \( \sin 2x = 2\sin x\cos x \), we have \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin 2x_{i} = 2\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin x_{i}\cos x_{i} \).

Since \( \sin x_{i} \) and \( \cos x_{i} \) are bounded between \( -1 \) and \( 1 \), we have \( \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin 2x_{i}\right| \leq 2\sum_{i=1}^{n} \left|\sin x_{i}\right|\left|\cos x_{i}\right| \).

By the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality, \( \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_{i}b_{i}\right)^{2} \leq \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_{i}^{2}\right)\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} b_{i}^{2}\right) \), we have \( \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} \left|\sin x_{i}\right|\left|\cos x_{i}\right|\right)^{2} \leq \sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin ^{2} x_{i} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \cos ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \cdot \frac{n}{2} = \frac{n^{2}}{4} \).

Thus, \( \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin 2x_{i}\right| \leq \sqrt{\frac{n^{2}}{4}} = \boxed{\frac{n}{2}} \).
